# Strategy

## Goal

Formalize Christian Merten's Jacobian challenge (`references/challenge.lean.ref`):
the nine protected declarations headed by `AlgebraicGeometry.Jacobian` and
`Jacobian.nonempty_jacobianWitness` — an Albanese/Jacobian object uniform over the
`k`-rational pointing of a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve `C/k`, with
**no** `C(k)≠∅` hypothesis and **no** `CharZero` (only `[Field k]`). The witness `J`
is built unconditionally; only `isAlbaneseFor` is quantified over the pointing.
Spine: **pointed vs. unpointed**, `J := Pic⁰_{C/k}`.

**Posture — option (c) holding pattern.** Forward the Route-A Picard substrate while
the Riemann–Roch substrate stays frozen by the USER ROUTE C PAUSE. No protected decl
yet closes kernel-clean; the nine typecheck modulo named sorry-axioms. The project is
early: the dominant Quot/Cartier engine and the A.3 tangent/Hilbert phases are
unstarted and rest on absent Mathlib infra. Near-term demonstrable milestone = honest
lower substrate (TS → RelPic) + the all-nine-typecheck skeleton.

## Phases & estimations

| Phase | Status | Iters left | LOC (remaining · realized/it) | Key Mathlib needs | Risks |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| A.1.c.SubT — line-bundle tensor group law (⊗-invertibility) | active; sole productive lane | ~4–7 | ~200–350 · ~0/it | monoidal structure on the **line-bundle (flat) subcategory** of `Scheme.Modules` (`J.W.IsMonoidal` for flat objects) | engine is moderate, NOT trivial: the iso-class associator needs strong-monoidal sheafification on the flat subcategory |
| A.1.c — RelPic functor | held (placeholder bodies) | ~3–5 post-SubT | ~50–150 · 0/it | A.1.c.SubT; ét-sheafify on `Over S` | RE-ENGAGE GATE: replace dishonest `PicSharp := const PUnit` + `functorial := 0` first |
| A.2.c — FGA Pic representability (typeclass scaffolding) | priority-2; RR-free | ~12–16 | ~600–800 · 0/it | A.1.c | does NOT include the Quot engine below |
| A.2.c-engine — Quot/Cartier construction | partial in QuotScheme.lean; **feasibility-gated** | ~30–60 | ~2000–4000 · ~0/it | Quot scheme (absent from Mathlib) | single largest build; **gated on a feasibility spike before further A.2.c LOC** |
| A.3.0/ii/vii — tangent + Pic⁰ AV-structure | gated A.2.c | ~26–45 | ~1100–2100 · 0/it | scheme tangent space; Hilbert poly | absent in Mathlib; likely under-counted |
| A.4 — Albanese UP (Route 2) | gated A.2.c | ~12–20 | ~600–1000 · 0/it | `rmk:Alb` (from representability) + autoduality `J^∨≅J` + Galois descent | autoduality RR-freeness UNVERIFIED (see Open questions) |
| genusZero + `nonempty_jacobianWitness` body | gated A.3 | ~5–7 | ~250–450 · 0/it | tangent-iso + connectedness | hidden A.2.c transit |

**Total Route A**: ~95–155 iters / ~3000–5500 LOC.

## Routes

`J := Pic⁰_{C/k}` (Kleiman §4–§5, Nitsure §5, Milne III §6). Bottom-up (USER directive):
capacity to ungated roots; never a gated target before its roots close; no A.3+ before
A.2.c. Every directive cites Kleiman/Nitsure/Milne/Mumford/Hartshorne/Stacks.

**Critical path to A.2.c** (all RR-free): A.1.c.SubT (group law) → A.1.c (RelPic functor)
→ A.2.c (representability).

**A.1.c.SubT — line-bundle group law via ⊗-invertibility.** The Picard group is the
group of units of the commutative monoid of ⊗-isomorphism-classes of invertible objects
of `Scheme.Modules`, mirroring Mathlib's `CommRing.Pic = Units (Skeleton (ModuleCat R))`
(`Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup`). A line object is one with `∃ N, M⊗N ≅ 𝒪`
(⊗-invertibility, Stacks 01HK); the inverse is carried by the predicate, so the geometric
local-triviality bookkeeping (`tensorObj_restrict_iso`, `exists_tensorObj_inverse`) drops
off the critical path. The remaining engine is the monoidal coherence (associativity of
⊗-iso-classes), which is **not** trivial: on the sheaf category it is strong-monoidal
sheafification, i.e. `J.W.IsMonoidal` (the gate on Mathlib's `CategoryTheory.Sheaf.monoidalCategory`,
`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Monoidal`). For ALL modules this is the `MonoidalClosed
(PresheafOfModules R₀)` wall; restricted to the **flat / line-bundle subcategory** it holds
by flat-exactness (`whiskerLeft : W g → W (F ◁ g)` for flat `F` via
`Module.Flat.lTensor_preserves_injective_linearMap`). So the monoidal structure is built on
the flat subcategory, where the coherence is available without `MonoidalClosed`. The `⊗`
object definition itself (`sheafification ∘ PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj`) is the
correct Mathlib shape and is retained. Rationale: `analogies/tsconstruct209.md`, `analogies/ts-design206.md`.

**A.2.c — representability is RR-free** (Nitsure §5 + Kleiman §4). Six Prop-valued typeclasses
with `⟨sorry⟩` constructors; downstream Route A proceeds under these. The 600–800 LOC budgets
ONLY the typeclass scaffolding — the construction discharging the `⟨sorry⟩`s is the
Quot/Cartier engine (its own phase row; sorry-axiomatized under option (c) until it lands).

**Albanese UP — Route 2.** The UP is derived from `Pic` representability via Kleiman `rmk:Alb`,
RR-free, on the dual `J^∨`; landing it on `J` uses the autoduality `J^∨≅J` (theta divisor /
Poincaré sheaf, Milne Thm 6.6 / EGK Thm 2.1) plus a Galois descent `k̄→k` (Milne Prop 6.4).
This supersedes the Route-1 Milne-Thm-3.2 codim cone (`Albanese/{CodimOneExtension,
Thm32RationalMapExtension, AuslanderBuchsbaum, CoheightBridge}.lean`), which is retained
**reversibly** — NOT deleted, NOT dead — until the deletion gate is met: (i) autoduality
RR-freeness independently re-verified (EGK Thm 2.1 read directly — the classical theta
divisor `W_{g-1}` rests on RR, so this is a genuine soundness risk); (ii) the ~20–30 LOC
valuative-criterion Route-1 repair sketch preserved in the blueprint.

**Route C — Riemann–Roch — PAUSED (USER directive).** Files stay imported with inline sorries
satisfied modulo option (c). Needed only at the three nodes named in Goal.

**Genus-0 arm.** (a) Route-A Pic⁰-via-AV-wrap (transits A.2.c); (b) direct `J := Spec k` via
Mumford rigidity — substrate partial, PAUSED under a USER amendment.

## Open strategic questions

- **Quot-engine feasibility is a HARD GATE on A.2.c.** Before further A.2.c LOC, a READ-ONLY
  spike (strategy-auditor on Nitsure §5 + a Mathlib-API survey) must confirm a Lean-expressible
  Quot shape on the current base, AND decompose the engine into concrete sub-phases (Quot
  functor → representability → flattening → Cartier). The project must not build ~50 iters of
  substrate around an unstarted, un-de-risked, absent-from-Mathlib crux.
- **Autoduality RR-freeness — second-verify before deleting the Route-1 cone** (EGK Thm 2.1
  read directly, not via Kleiman). The classical theta divisor rests on RR; if autoduality
  transits RR, Route 2 collides with the paused Route C.
- **Flat-subcategory monoidal structure — verify mechanical before dispatch.** Confirm that
  `whiskerLeft`-for-flat / `J.W.IsMonoidal` on the line-bundle subcategory is buildable from
  present Mathlib (`Module.Flat.lTensor_*`) and is not a renamed `MonoidalClosed` wall.
- **`k̄→k` Galois descent** sits at the no-`C(k)` heart of the challenge; verify per-pointing
  `isAlbaneseFor` composes with the descent + autoduality before treating it as minor.
- **`Pic⁰`: `degComp` vs `Pic^z`.** `degComp` identifies the component by degree (RR-dependent);
  `Pic^z` is RR-free but needs identity-component infra (~350 LOC). Decide near A.2.c.
- **USER goal-amendment** (cheapest→costliest): (a) carve Rigidity out of the pause → genus-0
  closure; (b) full Route C → unconditional end-state; (c) keep option-(c) (current).

## Mathlib gaps & new material

**Gaps to fill (Route A).**

- A.1.c.SubT: `IsInvertible` predicate; monoidal structure on the flat/line-bundle subcategory
  (`J.W.IsMonoidal` for flat objects); CommMonoid/Units group on ⊗-iso-classes (~200–350).
- A.1.c: ét-sheafify on `Over S`; `OnProduct` re-point to the `IsInvertible` subtype (~50–150).
- A.2.c: FGA Pic representability via Quot/Cartier (~600–800).
- A.3.0/ii/vii: scheme tangent space; Hilbert-poly + Pphifin; Pic⁰ AV-structure (~1100–2100).
- A.4 (Route 2): `rmk:Alb` UP from representability; autoduality `J^∨≅J`; Galois descent `k̄→k`.
- Connected étale group scheme over a field, dim 0 = `Spec k` (~50–100).

**New project material.** AbelianVarietyRigidity, RigidityLemma, Genus0BaseObjects/*,
RiemannRoch/* (paused), Picard/{RelativeSpec, LineBundlePullback, RelPicFunctor,
FGAPicRepresentability, IdentityComponent, Pic0AbelianVariety, QuotScheme,
FlatteningStratification, TensorObjSubstrate}, Albanese/AlbaneseUP. Route-1 cone
(Albanese/{CodimOneExtension, Thm32RationalMapExtension, AuslanderBuchsbaum, CoheightBridge})
retained reversibly behind the deletion gate.

## End-state overview

## Iter-209 — Lane TS construction pivot to ⊗-invertibility (no prover dispatch — structural-diagnosis iter)

unfolding" premise for `tensorObj_restrict_iso` was DISPROVEN (residual = opaque

**progress-critic ts209: STUCK** (all 4 triggers fire). Mandated corrective = Mathlib
analogy consult. **mathlib-analogist tsconstruct209** (`analogies/tsconstruct209.md`,
ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB critical): `tensorObj_restrict_iso` is an ARTIFACT of building the
group law on the *geometric* `IsLocallyTrivial` predicate. Mathlib's idiom is
**⊗-invertibility** (`Module.Invertible`, `CommRing.Pic = Units(Skeleton ModuleCat)`):
a line object is `∃ N, M⊗N≅𝒪`. Under it `tensorObj_restrict_iso` and
`exists_tensorObj_inverse` drop off the critical path. Reverts to the project's OWN
iter-174 intent (`LineBundlePullback.lean:50–58`); touches no protected decl.

**strategy-critic clean209: CHALLENGE (load-bearing).** The analogist's "3 mechanical
coherence isos via `sheafification.mapIso`" framing is imprecise: the iso-class
**associator** needs strong-monoidal sheafification (`sheafify A ⊗ sheafify B ≅
sheafify(A⊗B)`). Planner Mathlib check CONFIRMED: `CategoryTheory.Sheaf.monoidalCategory`
(`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Monoidal`) exists but is **gated on `[J.W.IsMonoidal]`**,
and `MonoidalCategory (SheafOfModules _)` returns NO results. So the monoidal engine is
NOT trivial — for all modules it is the `MonoidalClosed` wall; on the **flat/line-bundle
subcategory** it holds by flat-exactness (`whiskerLeft` for flat `F` via
`Module.Flat.lTensor_preserves_injective_linearMap`; `analogies/ts-design206.md` #2).

**Decision (full rationale: `iter/iter-209/plan.md`).** Pivot Lane TS to ⊗-invertibility
(destination confirmed) with the engine corrected to the flat-subcategory monoidal
structure. **NO prover dispatch this iter** — dispatching the analogist's imprecise
"mechanical isos" framing would be a 5th consecutive DISPROVEN; the corrected target
(flat-subcategory `J.W.IsMonoidal`) needs a blueprint engine-correction + scoped review
FIRST. This is the progress-critic's recommended Option B (consult-driven, zero dispatch).

### USER standing directives (active)

1. **ROUTE C PAUSE (permanent)**: all RR.* lanes + Rigidity/Genus0 substrate OFF-LIMITS. Lane RCI HELD.
2. **ROUTE A BOTTOM-UP**: ungated roots first; never a gated target before predecessors close.

   `∃ N, M⊗N≅𝒪`. Under it `tensorObj_restrict_iso` AND `exists_tensorObj_inverse` drop
   off the critical path. Reverts to the project's OWN documented iter-174 intent
   (`LineBundlePullback.lean:50–58`); touches no protected decl.

   coherence isos via `sheafification.mapIso`" framing is imprecise — the iso-class
   **associator** needs strong-monoidal sheafification. I VERIFIED via the LSP:
   `CategoryTheory.Sheaf.monoidalCategory` (`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Monoidal`) exists
   but is gated on `[J.W.IsMonoidal]`, and `MonoidalCategory (SheafOfModules _)` returns NO
   results. So the monoidal engine is NOT trivial: for all modules it is the `MonoidalClosed`
   wall; on the **flat / line-bundle subcategory** it holds by flat-exactness
   (`whiskerLeft`-for-flat, `analogies/ts-design206.md` #2). The pivot's destination is
   right; its engine is moderate, not mechanical.

Net: the pivot is committed (destination ⊗-invertibility, engine = flat-subcategory
monoidal). I did NOT dispatch a prover — the corrected target needs a blueprint
engine-correction + scoped review first; dispatching the imprecise framing would repeat
the DISPROVEN pattern. STRATEGY.md rewritten (user "make it cleaner" hint + pivot + all 4

**Fork** (progress-critic ts209 STUCK; iter-208 prover recommendation):

    decomposition (the prover's recommendation, starting from ingredient (4));

  "every prior 'most detailed decomposition' also ran into its first ingredient absent from
  Mathlib and fired DISPROVEN." Grinding the 4-ingredient chain is the COE-class

125	**The associator coherence iso is NOT the cheap ~15-LOC `sheafification.mapIso`

128	
129	- `tensorObj M N := sheafification(ptensor(M.val, N.val))`. Hence
130	  `tensorObj (tensorObj M N) P = sheafification(ptensor(sheafification(ptensor M.val N.val).val, P.val))`
131	  — the associator nests a sheafification **inside** the presheaf tensor on both
132	  sides. The unitors and braiding do **not** nest (one factor is the unit, or the

134	  `sheafification.mapIso(λ/ρ/β)` pattern genuinely works (~15 LOC, as the
135	  directive says, matching the existing green `tensorObj_unit_iso`).

137	  **sheafification–tensor absorption** iso

139	  (and its mirror): the sheafification unit `η_A` must become an iso after
140	  whiskering by an arbitrary module `B` and re-sheafifying.
141	- That is **exactly** the whiskering-stability `W g ⇒ W(F ◁ g)` for arbitrary
142	  `F` which the chapter's own `rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path` states is

146	  obstruction for the unitors/braiding and the inverse (genuine wins), but the
147	  **associator still bottoms out in the same absent infrastructure**, because the
148	  CommMonoid law must hold for *arbitrary* `M,N,P` (it is the monoid on iso-classes
149	  of ALL of `X.Modules`, units = invertibles, per the directive's
150	  `CommRing.Pic = Units(Skeleton(ModuleCat R))` template — but `ModuleCat R` has a
151	  genuine `MonoidalCategory` instance, which `X.Modules` lacks).
152	- I have written the chapter per the directive's construction (the design is
153	  sound), with the associator block carrying an explicit `% NOTE` and the LOC
154	  estimate flagging the absorption iso as the one piece that may need building
155	  first. **Recommendation:** before pushing a prover at `lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso`
156	  / the CommMonoid assembly, re-consult the mathlib-analogist on whether the

162	  iso-classes) is a residual gap, and consider building the group law only on the
163	  invertible (locally-free rank-1) subcategory where local trivialisations may
164	  give the associator directly.

Iter-209 plan phase complete. Here's the summary.

## Iter-209 — Lane TS construction pivot (no prover dispatch)

**Situation.** Lane TS — the sole USER-permitted productive lane and the critical-path root for the PRIMARY GOAL (A.2.c) — was confirmed **STUCK** by progress-critic ts209 (4 consecutive iters, 0 critical-path closures, the same absent-Mathlib wall renamed each iter). The iter-208 Route-A premise had been disproven exactly as its reversal signal predicted.

**Decision — pivot the construction, hold the prover.** Three consults drove it:

1. **mathlib-analogist (ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB, critical):** the 4-iter `tensorObj_restrict_iso` blocker is an *artifact* of building the group law on the geometric `IsLocallyTrivial` predicate. Mathlib's idiom is **⊗-invertibility** (`Module.Invertible` / `CommRing.Pic = Units(Skeleton)`): a line object is `∃N, M⊗N≅𝒪`. Under it, `tensorObj_restrict_iso` **and** `exists_tensorObj_inverse` drop off the critical path. This reverts to the project's own documented iter-174 intent and touches no protected declaration.

2. **strategy-critic (CHALLENGE) + my LSP check + the writer's finding (all convergent):** the analogist's "3 mechanical coherence isos" framing is imprecise — the iso-class **associator** nests sheafification and needs `J.W.IsMonoidal` (`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Monoidal.Sheaf.monoidalCategory` exists but is gated on it; `MonoidalCategory (SheafOfModules _)` has no instance). On *all* modules that's the `MonoidalClosed` wall; on the **invertible/line-bundle subcategory** it's available via local trivializations. So the pivot's destination is right; its engine is moderate, not trivial.

I therefore **did not dispatch a prover** (progress-critic's recommended Option B) — dispatching the imprecise framing would have been a 5th "almost there." The corrected target (subcategory associator) needs a blueprint engine-confirmation + scoped review first.

**Delivered this iter:**
- **STRATEGY.md** rewritten — cleaner (118 lines, down from 134; per-iter narrative scrubbed) per your hint, plus the pivot and all 4 strategy-critic must-fixes (engine corrected, Quot spike → hard gate, Route-1 reframed "retained reversibly, not dead").
- **TS blueprint chapter** rewritten to ⊗-invertibility with an honest associator caveat; correct Stacks citations (01CS/0B8K/01CX) fetched by a reference-retriever.
- PROGRESS.md, task_pending.md, and the iter-209 sidecar updated with a concrete iter-210 plan gated on confirming the subcategory associator is buildable without `MonoidalClosed`.

Build remains GREEN at 80 sorries (no Lean edits). One process note recorded: a concurrent-writer race (re-dispatching after a flaky-environment "failure" that was actually still running) — handled gracefully, but flagged to avoid recurrence.
